ReadThisFirst: This file is the machine-readable step interface catalog, not a run-plan JSON file.
Use Steps as the only step contract list in this file; do not infer extra steps from prose or examples.
Use RecommendedExecutionOrder as the default ordering guide when assembling a new plan.
Use docs/cli/json-plan.md for the surrounding run-plan file shape and variable syntax.
Use examples/json-plans/*.json for complete plan examples.
AgentUsageRules: Select a step by exact Kind.
Supply every input with Required=true unless the plan intentionally obtains it from prior step outputs or defaults documented elsewhere.
Treat Preconditions as operational requirements that must be satisfied before the step runs.
Treat VerificationNotes as evidence requirements for judging success.
Do not use tsproj.merge-fragment or generic tsproj.upsert-* when a dedicated tsproj primitive exists.

If needed behavior is missing from Steps, report the missing human operation, XML before/after shape, target path, field meanings, verification method, and suggested API name instead of inventing a hidden step.
SyncPolicy: docs/reference/step-catalog.json and docs/reference/step-catalog.md are generated companions from the same catalog source.
Do not hand-edit either generated file.
Any change to step specs or generated reference wording must regenerate both files in the same change.
If the JSON and Markdown disagree, treat the generated pair as stale and rerun generate-docs; do not merge the disagreement manually.
For public step contract changes, update TwinCatStepCatalog.cs, implementation/CLI/tests as needed, then run generate-docs.

StepFieldMeanings.Kind: Stable public step identifier used in invoke-step and JSON plan steps.
StepFieldMeanings.MethodName: Service method that implements the step behavior.
StepFieldMeanings.Category: High-level step family such as engineering, tsproj, signing, or validation.
StepFieldMeanings.Preconditions: Required state or environment before running the step.
StepFieldMeanings.Inputs: Request fields accepted by the step payload or direct CLI options.
StepFieldMeanings.Inputs.Required: True means the caller must provide a value unless a wrapper supplies it explicitly.
StepFieldMeanings.Inputs.Example: Scalar example for that field; it is not a full step payload.
StepFieldMeanings.Outputs: Named values exposed by the step outcome and available to later JSON plan steps.
StepFieldMeanings.VerificationNotes: Evidence or readback expected after running the step.

PlanAuthoringRules: A run-plan file has variables/files/steps structure from docs/cli/json-plan.md; this catalog only describes valid step kinds and payload fields.
Each plan step should reference one catalog Kind and pass a payload matching Inputs.
Prefer small explicit steps over macro commands when durable evidence or precise repair is needed.
Keep deviations from RecommendedExecutionOrder intentional and explain them in plan comments or evidence.

Steps.Summary: Creates a C++/header/resource/None project item, optionally creating the physical file and registering it in .vcxproj and .filters.
Preconditions: The target C++ .vcxproj must already exist.
RelativePath must be inside the project directory.

Inputs.Name: ProjectName
Inputs.Description: C++ project name.

Inputs.Name: RelativePath
Inputs.Description: Path relative to the C++ project directory.

Inputs.Name: ItemType
Inputs.Description: MSBuild item type, or Infer from extension.

Inputs.Name: Filter
Inputs.Description: Optional .vcxproj.filters display filter.

Inputs.Name: AddToProject
Inputs.Description: Whether to register the item in .vcxproj.

Inputs.Name: CreatePhysicalFile
Inputs.Description: Whether to create an empty physical file.

Inputs.Name: ConflictPolicy
Inputs.Description: FailIfExists, KeepExisting, or ReplaceProjectRegistration.

Inputs.Name: AllowMsBuildFallback
Inputs.Description: Allow typed MSBuild XML update when DTE ProjectItems is not stable.

Outputs.Name: addedToProject
Outputs.Description: Whether .vcxproj registration exists after the step.

VerificationNotes: Verify the file exists, .vcxproj contains the requested Include, .filters contains filter mapping when requested, and reopen shows the item.

Steps.Summary: Removes a C++ project item registration and optional filter entry and physical file.
Preconditions: The target C++ .vcxproj must already exist.

Inputs.Name: ProjectName
Inputs.Description: C++ project name.

Inputs.Name: RelativePath
Inputs.Description: Path relative to the C++ project directory.

Inputs.Name: ItemType
Inputs.Description: MSBuild item type, or Infer from extension.

Inputs.Name: DeletePhysicalFile
Inputs.Description: Whether to delete the physical file.

Inputs.Name: RemoveFilterEntry
Inputs.Description: Whether to remove the .vcxproj.filters item mapping.

Inputs.Name: IgnoreMissing
Inputs.Description: Treat missing item/file as success.

Outputs.Name: deletedFile
Outputs.Description: Whether a physical file was deleted.

VerificationNotes: Verify .vcxproj/.filters no longer reference the item and the file is absent when DeletePhysicalFile=true.

Steps.Summary: Sets a C++ ItemDefinitionGroup tool property such as ClCompile include paths or Link dependencies.
Preconditions: The target C++ .vcxproj must already exist.

Inputs.Name: ProjectName
Inputs.Description: C++ project name.

Inputs.Name: ToolName

Inputs.Name: PropertyName
Inputs.Description: Tool property element name.

Inputs.Name: Value
Inputs.Description: Property value, including inherited macros when required.

Inputs.Name: Condition
Inputs.Description: Optional ItemDefinitionGroup Condition.

VerificationNotes: Inspect ItemDefinitionGroup XML and confirm build logs use include paths, library paths, language standard, dependencies, or events.

Steps.Summary: Sets file-level MSBuild metadata for a C++ project item, such as PrecompiledHeader or ExcludedFromBuild.
Preconditions: The target item must already be registered in .vcxproj.

Inputs.Name: ProjectName
Inputs.Description: C++ project name.

Inputs.Name: RelativePath
Inputs.Description: Path relative to the C++ project directory.

Inputs.Name: ItemType
Inputs.Description: MSBuild item type containing the item.

Inputs.Name: MetadataName
Inputs.Description: Metadata element name.

Inputs.Name: Value
Inputs.Description: Metadata value.

Inputs.Name: Condition
Inputs.Description: Optional item Condition.

VerificationNotes: Inspect the item XML and confirm build output respects include/exclude or PCH metadata.

Steps.Summary: Sets a project-level or configuration-level MSBuild property in a C++ .vcxproj.
Preconditions: The target C++ .vcxproj must already exist.

Inputs.Name: ProjectName
Inputs.Description: C++ project name.

Inputs.Name: PropertyName
Inputs.Description: MSBuild property element name.

Inputs.Name: Value
Inputs.Description: Property value.

Inputs.Name: Condition
Inputs.Description: Optional PropertyGroup Condition.

Inputs.Name: PropertyGroupLabel
Inputs.Description: Optional PropertyGroup Label, such as Globals or Configuration.

VerificationNotes: Inspect .vcxproj and confirm the PropertyGroup contains the requested value; build log should reflect build-affecting properties.

Steps.Summary: Writes source/resource/text payload into an existing C++ project item file and returns its content hash.
Preconditions: The target C++ project must already exist.
ContentText and ContentFile are mutually exclusive.

Inputs.Name: ProjectName
Inputs.Description: C++ project name.

Inputs.Name: RelativePath
Inputs.Description: Path relative to the C++ project directory.

Inputs.Name: ContentText
Inputs.Description: Inline source payload.

Inputs.Name: ContentFile
Inputs.Description: Payload file path produced by the JSON plan files[] section or another explicit caller-owned payload.

Inputs.Name: Encoding
Inputs.Description: utf-8, utf-8-bom, or ascii.

Inputs.Name: NewLine
Inputs.Description: preserve, crlf, or lf.

Inputs.Name: WritePolicy
Inputs.Description: FailIfMissing, FailIfNonEmpty, or Overwrite.

Inputs.Name: RequireProjectRegistration
Inputs.Description: Fail if .vcxproj does not already register the item.

Outputs.Name: sha256
Outputs.Description: SHA256 hash of the written bytes.

VerificationNotes: Compare the output hash with the payload and reopen the item in VS.

Steps.Summary: Saves the current configuration archive when possible and then activates TwinCAT via ITcSysManager; DTE command fallback is opt-in for interactive troubleshooting.
Preconditions: A solution must be loaded and its ITcSysManager must be available.

Inputs.Name: SaveConfigurationArchive
Inputs.Description: Whether to attempt SaveConfiguration before activate.

Inputs.Name: ConfigurationArchivePath
Inputs.Description: Optional override for the generated .tszip path.

Inputs.Name: SuppressUi
Inputs.Description: Whether DTE.SuppressUI should be enabled before activation.

Inputs.Name: AllowDteCommandFallback
Inputs.Description: Whether activation may fall back to DTE ExecuteCommand names that can show interactive prompts.

Inputs.Name: ActivationTimeoutMs
Inputs.Description: Maximum wall-clock time for ActivateConfiguration plus StartRestartTwinCAT before failing unattended.

Outputs.Name: activationCommand
Outputs.Description: The command or fallback path used for activation.

VerificationNotes: Do not treat this step alone as runtime proof; follow with ADS state assertions and TcSysSrv error-window checks.

Steps.Summary: Adds a TcCOM instance from a project TMC by resolving the module GUID and calling CreateChild on the project node.
Preconditions: The target C++ project and its .tmc file must exist.

Inputs.Name: ProjectName
Inputs.Description: Owning C++ project name.

Inputs.Name: ProjectTmcPath
Inputs.Description: Absolute path to the project TMC.

Inputs.Name: InstanceBaseName
Inputs.Description: Base name to use when creating the instance.

Inputs.Name: ModuleClassName
Inputs.Description: Optional module class name when the TMC contains multiple classes.

Inputs.Name: AllowOfflineFallback
Inputs.Description: Whether AddModuleInstance may inject a deterministic instance skeleton through .tsproj file mutation when COM CreateChild fails.

Outputs.Name: treeItemPath
Outputs.Description: TwinCAT tree path for the instance.

VerificationNotes: Export the instance XML and confirm that the expected CLSID and Name are present.

Steps.Summary: Applies a batch IO tree payload by orchestrating engineering.create-io-device and engineering.create-ethercat-box operations.
Preconditions: A bound XAE solution must be open.
The payload is a convenience wrapper around CreateChild operations only; it must not contain .tsproj XML metadata.
Use this for large IO trees, then save/export/describe topology to prove what XAE generated.

Inputs.Name: Devices
Inputs.Description: IO device CreateChild requests to apply in order.

Inputs.Name: Boxes
Inputs.Description: EtherCAT box/terminal CreateChild requests to apply in order.

Outputs.Name: treeItemPaths
Outputs.Description: Semicolon-separated tree paths returned by XAE.

Outputs.Name: nodesJson
Outputs.Description: Serialized node info returned by the underlying CreateChild operations.

VerificationNotes: Run tsproj.describe-io-topology or export TIID after SaveAll; this wrapper does not prove PDO/process-image parity by itself.

Steps.Summary: Applies a structured JSON module model to a TwinCAT C++ project .tmc without copying a whole known-good TMC file.
Preconditions: The target project .tmc must already exist.
GeneratedServicesHeaderPath should point at the project Services.h; GeneratedHeaderPaths can add companion headers such as Interfaces.h for custom interface GUIDs.

Inputs.Name: ProjectTmcPath
Inputs.Description: Absolute path to the project .tmc file.

Inputs.Name: ProjectName
Inputs.Description: TwinCAT C++ project / class factory name.

Inputs.Name: Modules
Inputs.Description: Structured module definitions containing GUIDs, interfaces, parameters, DataAreas, interface pointers, data pointers, and event classes.

Inputs.Name: GeneratedServicesHeaderPath
Inputs.Description: Optional generated Services.h path used to resolve custom type GUIDs and DataTypes.

Inputs.Name: GeneratedHeaderPaths
Inputs.Description: Optional additional generated headers used to resolve companion custom type or interface GUIDs.

Inputs.Name: LibraryName
Inputs.Description: Optional Library/Name override. Defaults to ProjectName.

Inputs.Name: LibraryVersion
Inputs.Description: Library/Version value.

Inputs.Name: RemoveUnexpectedModules
Inputs.Description: Whether module entries not present in Modules should be removed.

Inputs.Name: ReplaceDataTypesFromGeneratedHeader
Inputs.Description: Whether generated header DataTypes should replace the .tmc DataTypes section.

VerificationNotes: Run engineering.verify-tmc-data-areas afterwards and inspect the .tmc DataTypes/Modules sections; this step must not receive raw module XML fragments.

Steps.Summary: Builds the loaded solution through DTE, an unattended devenv.com command-line build, or an MSBuild project sequence.
Preconditions: DTE engine requires a loaded solution.
CommandLine engine requires a solution path and installed Visual Studio/XAE command-line build support.
MsBuildProjects engine requires C++ project paths that can be built outside the XAE solution shell.

Inputs.Name: TimeoutMs
Inputs.Description: Maximum build wait time.

Inputs.Name: BuildEngine
Inputs.Description: Build backend: Dte, CommandLine, or MsBuildProjects.

Inputs.Name: Configuration
Inputs.Description: Solution configuration for command-line build.

Inputs.Name: Platform
Inputs.Description: Solution platform for command-line build.

Inputs.Name: DevenvPath
Inputs.Description: Optional explicit devenv.com path for command-line build.

Inputs.Name: MsBuildPath
Inputs.Description: Optional explicit MSBuild.exe path for MSBuildProjects engine.

Inputs.Name: ProjectPaths
Inputs.Description: Semicolon-separated C++ project path sequence for MSBuildProjects engine. Relative paths are resolved from the solution directory.

Inputs.Name: LogFilePath
Inputs.Description: Optional devenv /Out log destination.

Outputs.Name: lastBuildInfo
Outputs.Description: DTE LastBuildInfo value, or command-line exit code for CommandLine engine.

Outputs.Name: exitCode
Outputs.Description: Process exit code when CommandLine engine is used.

VerificationNotes: Treat LastBuildInfo == 0 or process exit code 0 as the engineering success condition.
For unattended runs, CommandLine and MsBuildProjects avoid Visual Studio confirmation dialogs blocking DTE automation.

Steps.Summary: Lists or explicitly kills unattended Visual Studio/TcXaeShell host processes that can block DTE automation.
Preconditions: Use dry-run first.
Default matching only targets host processes without a main window title; windowed IDEs require IncludeWindowed=true or explicit ProcessIds.

Inputs.Name: ProcessNames
Inputs.Description: Process names to inspect, separated by ';' or '|'.

Inputs.Name: ProcessIds
Inputs.Description: Optional explicit process ids to match.

Inputs.Name: DryRun
Inputs.Description: Whether to only report candidates without killing them.

Inputs.Name: IncludeWindowed
Inputs.Description: Whether processes with a main window title are also candidates.

Inputs.Name: KillProcessTree
Inputs.Description: Whether to kill the full process tree for matched processes.

Outputs.Name: killedCount
Outputs.Description: Number of processes killed when DryRun=false.

VerificationNotes: Run before unattended DTE launch if previous tests left headless devenv/TcXaeShell processes; follow with engineering.launch-visual-studio.

Steps.Summary: Closes the current Visual Studio DTE session, optionally issuing SaveAll first.
Preconditions: A live DTE session must exist.

Inputs.Name: SaveBeforeClose
Inputs.Description: Whether SaveAll should run before closing the session.

VerificationNotes: Confirm that the DTE process exits and the session object is no longer usable.

Steps.Summary: Creates a TwinCAT C++ project beneath TIXC using a specified Beckhoff wizard id.
Preconditions: A bound TwinCAT engineering session must exist.

Inputs.Name: ProjectName
Inputs.Description: Requested C++ project name.

Inputs.Name: WizardId
Inputs.Description: Wizard identifier for the project template.

Outputs.Name: treeItemPath
Outputs.Description: TwinCAT tree path for the C++ project.

VerificationNotes: Verify the TIXC child exists and the .vcxproj file lands on disk.

Steps.Summary: Creates an EtherCAT box or terminal under an EtherCAT parent through ITcSmTreeItem.CreateChild.
Preconditions: An EtherCAT device or parent box path must already exist.
Use subtype 9099 for E-Bus terminals/boxes whose product identity comes from ProductRevision/vInfo.
This step asks XAE/ESI to generate box metadata; it must not be replaced by copied sample XML.

Inputs.Name: ParentTreeItemPath
Inputs.Description: TwinCAT parent path, for example TIID^Device 3 (EtherCAT).

Inputs.Name: Name
Inputs.Description: Box or terminal display name to create or find.

Inputs.Name: SubType
Inputs.Description: TwinCAT CreateChild subtype. 9099 is the Automation Interface subtype for product-revision based EtherCAT boxes.

Inputs.Name: Before
Inputs.Description: Optional sibling name/path passed to CreateChild before insertion.

Inputs.Name: ProductRevision
Inputs.Description: Product/revision identity passed as vInfo when VInfo is not set, for example EK1100-0000-0017.

Inputs.Name: VInfo
Inputs.Description: Optional raw CreateChild vInfo string when Beckhoff documents a different identity payload.

Inputs.Name: Disabled
Inputs.Description: Optional Disabled state to apply after creation or lookup.

Inputs.Name: AllowExisting
Inputs.Description: Whether an existing child at ParentTreeItemPath^Name is accepted instead of failing.

Inputs.Name: PostCreateDelayMs
Inputs.Description: Delay after CreateChild so XAE can generate PDO/SyncMan/FMMU metadata.

Outputs.Name: treeItemPath
Outputs.Description: Created or existing tree item path.

Outputs.Name: objectId
Outputs.Description: Tree item ObjectId when XAE exposes one.

VerificationNotes: Export or describe the EtherCAT tree after SaveAll; PDO/process-image metadata should come from XAE/ESI generation rather than a copied .tsproj fragment.

Steps.Summary: Creates an IO device under a TwinCAT tree parent through ITcSmTreeItem.CreateChild.
Preconditions: A bound XAE solution must be open.
This is an engineering COM/XAE operation; use command timeouts and unattended dialog auto-dismiss for headless runs.
For an EtherCAT master use ParentTreeItemPath=TIID and SubType=111.

Inputs.Name: Name
Inputs.Description: Device display name to create or find.

Inputs.Name: SubType
Inputs.Description: TwinCAT CreateChild subtype, for example 111 for an EtherCAT master.

Inputs.Name: ParentTreeItemPath
Inputs.Description: TwinCAT tree parent path.

Inputs.Name: Before
Inputs.Description: Optional sibling name/path passed to CreateChild before insertion.

Inputs.Name: VInfo
Inputs.Description: Optional CreateChild vInfo payload for device-specific identity fields.

Inputs.Name: Disabled
Inputs.Description: Optional Disabled state to apply after creation or lookup.

Inputs.Name: AllowExisting
Inputs.Description: Whether an existing child at ParentTreeItemPath^Name is accepted instead of failing.

Inputs.Name: PostCreateDelayMs
Inputs.Description: Delay after CreateChild so XAE can persist generated IO metadata.

Outputs.Name: treeItemPath
Outputs.Description: Created or existing tree item path.

Outputs.Name: objectId
Outputs.Description: Tree item ObjectId when XAE exposes one.

VerificationNotes: Export or describe TIID after SaveAll; the created Device should appear without copying a Project/Io XML section.

Steps.Summary: Creates a PLC project using the first compatible Beckhoff PLC template available on the machine.

Steps.Summary: Creates a fresh TwinCAT XAE solution and binds the automation session to its ITcSysManager root.

Steps.Summary: Exports ProduceXml output for any TwinCAT tree node as durable evidence.

Steps.Summary: Invokes TwinCAT GenerateMappings so XAE can rebuild variable mappings from the current IO tree.
Preconditions: A bound XAE solution with IO topology must be open.
The step first requires ITcSmCommands.GenerateMappings on the current SysManager COM object.
DTE command fallback is disabled by default because menu commands can show interactive prompts.

Inputs.Name: SuppressUi
Inputs.Description: Whether DTE.SuppressUI should be set before invoking the command.

Inputs.Name: AllowDteCommandFallback
Inputs.Description: Whether to try TwinCAT GenerateMappings DTE commands if ITcSmCommands is not available.

Inputs.Name: TimeoutMs
Inputs.Description: Maximum time allowed for the GenerateMappings operation.

Outputs.Name: command
Outputs.Description: The COM or DTE command path that completed.

VerificationNotes: Save and describe/compare the IO topology and root Mappings after this step; do not treat command success alone as IO parity.

Steps.Summary: Starts or attaches a DTE session that later steps can use for XAE creation, build, and activation.
Preconditions: Visual Studio with TwinCAT XAE must be installed on the machine.

Inputs.Name: ProgId
Inputs.Description: Visual Studio DTE ProgId.

Inputs.Name: StartupDelayMs
Inputs.Description: Warm-up delay before the DTE session is used.

Inputs.Name: Visible
Inputs.Description: Whether the launched Visual Studio window should be visible.

Inputs.Name: SuppressUi
Inputs.Description: Whether DTE.SuppressUI should be enabled for unattended runs.

Inputs.Name: LaunchTimeoutMs
Inputs.Description: Maximum time allowed for DTE COM activation before failing the step.

Inputs.Name: EnableDialogAutoDismiss
Inputs.Description: Whether unattended runs should monitor selected VS/TcXaeShell host processes during DTE COM activation, fallback launch, startup delay, and the active session, then close known modal confirmation dialogs by title or message text.

Inputs.Name: DialogPollIntervalMs
Inputs.Description: Polling interval for the unattended dialog auto-dismiss watcher.

Inputs.Name: AttachToExisting
Inputs.Description: Whether launch may attach to an already running DTE session. Keep false for unattended runs so stale headless Visual Studio hosts are not reused.

Inputs.Name: RootSuffix
Inputs.Description: Optional Visual Studio /RootSuffix used by explicit fallback launch to isolate a broken user registry hive or profile during unattended DTE startup.

Inputs.Name: DteHostPath
Inputs.Description: Optional explicit devenv.exe or TcXaeShell.exe path used for fallback DTE host launch when COM activation does not return promptly.

Inputs.Name: PreferDteHostLaunch
Inputs.Description: Whether to start the explicit DTE host first and attach through the ROT before attempting Activator.CreateInstance. Useful when COM activation is known to show an unattended registry/profile error.

Steps.Summary: Re-opens an existing TwinCAT solution and re-attaches COM references after .tsproj file mutations.

Preconditions: A live DTE session must exist.
VerificationNotes: Re-check file timestamps or solution dirtiness after the command.

Steps.Summary: Invokes TwinCAT SearchDevices through ITcSmCommands so XAE can scan local IO devices without using menu commands.
Preconditions: A bound XAE solution must be open.
This operation can change the IO tree and can depend on local hardware/driver state.
No DTE menu fallback is provided; unattended runs must rely on the COM command, timeout, suppress-ui, and dialog auto-dismiss.

Inputs.Name: SuppressUi
Inputs.Description: Whether DTE.SuppressUI should be set before invoking the command.

Inputs.Name: TimeoutMs
Inputs.Description: Maximum time allowed for SearchDevices.

Outputs.Name: command
Outputs.Description: The COM command path that completed.

VerificationNotes: Save, export TIID, and compare/describe IO topology after this step. A successful scan command is not enough to claim OptCNC IO parity.

Steps.Summary: Invokes the TwinCAT C++ project StartTmcCodeGenerator method so source annotations regenerate TMC metadata.
Preconditions: The target TwinCAT C++ project must exist and expose StartTmcCodeGenerator in its tree XML.

Inputs.Name: ProjectName
Inputs.Description: TwinCAT C++ project name.

Inputs.Name: PostStartDelayMs
Inputs.Description: Delay after triggering StartTmcCodeGenerator.

Inputs.Name: WaitForUpdatedTmcTimeoutMs
Inputs.Description: Maximum wait for the project .tmc timestamp to update.

Outputs.Name: updatedTmcPath
Outputs.Description: Project .tmc path observed after code generation.

Outputs.Name: succeeded
Outputs.Description: Whether code generation left a readable project .tmc.

Outputs.Name: updated
Outputs.Description: Whether the .tmc timestamp or content changed during this code generation call.

VerificationNotes: Check that the .tmc is readable and contains source-derived DataAreas, Parameters, Interfaces, and module classes; updated=false can still be acceptable if content was already current.

Steps.Summary: Reads a TwinCAT C++ .tmc file and verifies expected module DataAreas and symbols before instances are created.
Preconditions: Run engineering.start-tmc-code-generator and engineering.publish-modules first when source annotations were just written.

Inputs.Name: ProjectTmcPath
Inputs.Description: Absolute path to the project .tmc file.

Inputs.Name: Modules
Inputs.Description: Expected module names, DataAreas, AreaTypes, and symbols.

Inputs.Name: FailOnUnexpectedModule
Inputs.Description: Whether modules not listed in Modules should fail verification.

Outputs.Name: expectedModuleCount
Outputs.Description: Expected module count from the request.

Outputs.Name: errorsJson
Outputs.Description: Detailed mismatch list when verification fails.

VerificationNotes: Use this after C++ code generation and before add-module-instance; a fallback skeleton TMC with Input/DataIn and Output/DataOut should fail this step.

Steps.Summary: Asserts that EtherCAT productRevision strings used for CreateChild are present in the installed Beckhoff ESI/device-description XML files.
Preconditions: The machine must have Beckhoff EtherCAT device description XML files installed.
This step is file-only; it does not launch XAE and does not mutate a .tsproj.
Use it before engineering.create-ethercat-box or engineering.apply-io-tree-plan when a JSON plan depends on productRevision/vInfo values.

Inputs.Name: ProductRevisions
Inputs.Description: ProductRevision/vInfo values to verify, such as EK1100-0000-0018 or AX5125-0000-0214.

Inputs.Name: Items
Inputs.Description: Structured productRevision checks with optional ProductCode and RevisionNo constraints.

Inputs.Name: SearchDirectories
Inputs.Description: Optional EtherCAT ESI XML directories. Defaults to Beckhoff TwinCAT install/program-data locations.

Inputs.Name: IncludeHiddenTypes
Inputs.Description: Whether HideType elements should count as matches.

Outputs.Name: succeeded
Outputs.Description: Whether all requested product revisions were found.

Outputs.Name: scannedFileCount
Outputs.Description: Number of ESI XML files scanned.

Outputs.Name: assertionsJson
Outputs.Description: Per-product match result including ProductCode, RevisionNo and source file.

VerificationNotes: Run before XAE CreateChild IO tree steps; a match proves the local ESI catalog contains the requested productRevision string, but final IO topology still needs XAE tree and .tsproj guards.

Steps.Summary: Reads a TwinCAT Scope .tcscopex configuration and asserts typed channel/chart shape without mutating it.

Inputs.Name: ChartChannels
Inputs.Description: Expected chart channel names and optional acquisition names.

Outputs.Description: Whether every requested shape condition matched.

VerificationNotes: Use after scope.ensure-configuration or after XAE reopen/save to prove the generated channels were retained.

Steps.Summary: Creates or updates a TwinCAT Scope .tcscopex configuration from typed chart, channel, and ADS symbol definitions.

Callers must provide typed JSON or explicit fields; do not pass copied .tcscopex XML.

Inputs.Name: ReplaceChannels

Inputs.Name: AdsChannels

Inputs.Name: ChartChannels

Outputs.Name: chartChannelCount

VerificationNotes: Re-read the .tcscopex file and assert the requested ADS symbol channels and chart channels exist; do not compare against a copied sample file.

Steps.Summary: Grants or removes local TcSignTool authorization for a TwinCAT signing certificate.

Inputs.Name: Password
Inputs.Description: Certificate password. Prefer CLI password-file or password-env-var input; it is never emitted in step outputs.

Inputs.Name: RemoveGrant
Inputs.Description: Remove the local grant instead of adding it.

Inputs.Name: Quiet
Inputs.Description: Pass /q to TcSignTool.

Inputs.Name: ToolPath
Inputs.Description: Optional TcSignTool.exe override.

Outputs.Name: commandLine
Outputs.Description: Redacted command line used for evidence.

VerificationNotes: Run signing.sign-twincat-binary and signing.verify-twincat-binary after grant to close the loop.

Steps.Summary: Writes TwinCAT C++ project signing license settings used by MSBuild/TcSignTool.
Preconditions: The C++ .vcxproj file must exist.
Run this before engineering.build-solution so the generated .tmx is signed during build.

Inputs.Name: ProjectFilePath
Inputs.Description: Path to the C++ .vcxproj file, or resolve it from ProjectPath and CppProjectName.

Inputs.Name: LicenseName
Inputs.Description: TwinCAT signing certificate/license name written to TcSignTwinCatCertName.

Inputs.Name: Password

VerificationNotes: Inspect the C++ .vcxproj or generated TcSignTwinCatCmd.txt after build; do not expect ADS/runtime state to change until the project is rebuilt and activated.

Steps.Summary: Signs one or more built TwinCAT C++ binaries with Beckhoff TcSignTool.
Preconditions: engineering.build-solution must have produced the target .tmx/.sys binary.
The certificate file must exist on this machine.

Inputs.Name: CertificatePath
Inputs.Description: Path to the .tccert certificate file.

Inputs.Name: TargetPaths
Inputs.Description: One or more TwinCAT binary paths to sign.

Inputs.Name: Password
Inputs.Description: Certificate password. Prefer CLI password-file or password-env-var input; it is piped to TcSignTool instead of placed on the process command line.

Inputs.Name: Quiet
Inputs.Description: Pass /q to TcSignTool.

Inputs.Name: ToolPath
Inputs.Description: Optional TcSignTool.exe override.

Outputs.Name: commandLine
Outputs.Description: Redacted command line used for evidence.

VerificationNotes: Run signing.verify-twincat-binary and then activate the configuration with the signed binary.

Steps.Summary: Verifies that one or more TwinCAT binaries carry a valid TcSignTool signature.
Preconditions: The target TwinCAT binary must already exist.

Inputs.Name: TargetPaths
Inputs.Description: One or more TwinCAT binary paths to verify.

Inputs.Name: Quiet
Inputs.Description: Pass /q to TcSignTool.

Inputs.Name: ToolPath
Inputs.Description: Optional TcSignTool.exe override.

Inputs.Name: AllowTestModeWarning
Inputs.Description: Treat TcSignTool exit code 2 as success when the output says the file has a signature but the OEM certificate is not signed by Beckhoff.

Outputs.Name: acceptedTestModeWarning
Outputs.Description: Whether a TcSignTool test-mode certificate warning was accepted.

Outputs.Name: commandLine
Outputs.Description: Redacted command line used for evidence.

VerificationNotes: Treat exit code 0 as signed/verified; for local test certificates, set AllowTestModeWarning only when test-mode activation is acceptable.
Keep the signed binary path as activation evidence.

Steps.Summary: Applies a batch of instance data pointer writes in one deterministic .tsproj mutation pass.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and verify DataPointerValues entries for each requested instance/pointer pair.

Steps.Summary: Applies a batch of instance interface pointer writes in one deterministic .tsproj mutation pass.
Preconditions: Each referenced instance must already exist in the .tsproj.

Inputs.Name: Items
Inputs.Description: Batch entries containing InstanceName, PointerName, and ObjectId.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and verify InterfacePointerValues entries for each requested instance/pointer pair.

Steps.Summary: Applies a batch of instance parameter writes in one deterministic .tsproj mutation pass.
Preconditions: Each referenced instance must already exist in the .tsproj.

Inputs.Description: Batch entries containing InstanceName, ParameterName, and optional Value/Enum/String payloads.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and confirm each target instance received the expected ParameterValues updates.

Steps.Summary: Applies a batch IO topology payload by orchestrating dedicated IO Device, Box, PDO, MappingInfo, and Link primitives.
Preconditions: The payload must use structured dedicated fields and may only use documented raw fragments for known-good XML gaps.
Device entries must precede Boxes logically; the service applies Devices, Boxes, BoxImages, Pdos, MappingInfos, then Links.

Inputs.Name: Boxes
Inputs.Description: Box definitions to ensure.

Inputs.Name: Pdos

Inputs.Name: BoxImages

Inputs.Name: MappingInfos

Inputs.Name: Links
Inputs.Description: Mapping links to ensure.

Inputs.Name: EnsureIoSection

Outputs.Name: boxCount
Outputs.Description: Number of Box entries applied from the payload.

Outputs.Name: pdoCount

Steps.Summary: Applies generic element and fragment upserts in one deterministic .tsproj mutation pass.

Inputs.Name: FragmentUpserts
Inputs.Description: Fragment upserts to apply in order.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and verify all requested low-level mutations landed; FailOnConflict aborts the plan.

Steps.Summary: Reads a .tsproj and asserts that C++ instance DataPointerValues and root Mappings links still match the requested shape.
Preconditions: Use after data pointer and mapping steps, and again after XAE save/activate, to catch TwinCAT deleting unresolved DataPointerValues.

Inputs.Name: InstanceName
Inputs.Description: Exact C++ instance display name to inspect.

Inputs.Name: DataPointers
Inputs.Description: Required DataPointerValues entries, optional per-entry record count, and optional ArrayIndex set.

Inputs.Name: ExpectedDataPointerRecordCount
Inputs.Description: Expected total number of data pointer records across all DataPointerValues entries.

Inputs.Name: MappingLinks
Inputs.Description: Root Mappings OwnerA/OwnerB/Link entries that must be present.

Inputs.Name: ExpectedDataPointerMappingLinkCount
Inputs.Description: Expected number of root Mappings Link entries whose VarA or VarB is a Data Pointer reference.

Inputs.Name: ExpectedRootMappingLinkCount
Inputs.Description: Expected total root Mappings Link count.

Outputs.Name: succeeded
Outputs.Description: Whether all requested shape assertions passed.

Outputs.Name: dataPointerRecordCount
Outputs.Description: Total DataPointerValues record count for the inspected instance.

Outputs.Name: dataPointerMappingLinkCount
Outputs.Description: Root Mappings Link count limited to Data Pointer links.

Outputs.Name: shapeJson
Outputs.Description: Full observed shape and error details.

VerificationNotes: Run against the generated .tsproj before activation and after activation/save; for OptCNC, AxesGroup0 should retain six data pointer records and eight data pointer mapping links.

Steps.Summary: Reads a .tsproj and asserts IO process-image references without mutating TwinCAT metadata.
Preconditions: Use after IO topology creation to catch half-populated process-image shape, for example Device InfoImageId without a direct Image node or ImageId values with no known backing.

Inputs.Description: Expected root TcSmProject/ImageDatas/ImageData count.

Inputs.Name: ExpectedDeviceImageCount
Inputs.Description: Expected direct Device/Image count under Project/Io.

Inputs.Name: ExpectedImageReferenceCount

Inputs.Name: RequireDeviceImageForInfoImageId
Inputs.Description: Fail when a Device has InfoImageId but no direct Image node.

Inputs.Description: Fail when an ImageId does not match root ImageData, direct Device Image, Device InfoImageId, or AllowedUnbackedImageIds.

Inputs.Name: AllowedUnbackedImageIds
Inputs.Description: Known system image ids that are valid without root ImageData, for example TwinSAFE module image id 118.

Outputs.Name: succeeded
Outputs.Description: Whether all requested IO image reference assertions passed.

Outputs.Name: deviceInfoWithoutImageCount

VerificationNotes: For OptCNC sample parity, require four direct Device/Image nodes and allow only documented system image ids as unbacked.

Steps.Summary: Compares two .tsproj files through normalized IO topology facts and reports stable count/key/field differences.

Inputs.Description: Whether normalized attributes should be included in the underlying descriptions.

Inputs.Name: MaxDifferences
Inputs.Description: Maximum number of differences to return; zero means no cap.

Outputs.Name: succeeded
Outputs.Description: Whether all compared IO topology facts match.

Outputs.Name: truncated
Outputs.Description: Whether differences were truncated by MaxDifferences.

Outputs.Name: comparisonJson
Outputs.Description: Count comparisons and stable topology differences.

VerificationNotes: Use after generation and again after XAE save/activate; it compares process-image and PDO-entry facts as normalized fields, so a mismatch proves IO parity is not complete without requiring or exposing sample metadata XML.

The symbol paths, data types, and AMS endpoint must already be known.

Inputs.Name: NetId
Inputs.Description: AMS NetId, for example 127.0.0.1.1.1.

Inputs.Name: Port
Inputs.Description: ADS port, for example 851.

Inputs.Name: Symbols
Inputs.Description: Symbols to read; direct CLI accepts --symbols=MAIN.nSeed:UInt32;MAIN.bPipelineOk:Boolean or --json-file.

Inputs.Name: AutoReconnect
Inputs.Description: Whether to force a TwinCAT reconnect attempt before reading.

Inputs.Name: ContinueOnError
Inputs.Description: Whether the step should succeed when one or more symbols fail.

Outputs.Name: failedCount
Outputs.Description: Number of symbols that failed to read.

Outputs.Name: valuesText
Outputs.Description: Human-readable symbol=value list printed in the step result.

Outputs.Name: valuesJson
Outputs.Description: Per-symbol ADS read results including data types and errors.

VerificationNotes: Use after activation and ADS port scan; treat the printed values plus expected domain assertions as runtime proof.

Steps.Summary: Scans ADS target ports and reports whether the runtime endpoint is reachable before symbol-level ADS reads are attempted.
Preconditions: TwinCAT runtime or router must be installed on the target machine.
Use this before symbol reads when activation or ADS routing is uncertain.

Inputs.Name: NetId
Inputs.Description: AMS NetId, or local for the local router.

Inputs.Name: Ports
Inputs.Description: Comma-separated ADS ports to probe.

Outputs.Name: openPortCount
Outputs.Description: Number of ports with readable ADS state.

Outputs.Name: failedPortCount
Outputs.Description: Number of ports that failed to connect or read state.

Outputs.Name: portsJson
Outputs.Description: Per-port ADS state or error message.

VerificationNotes: If 100 or 10000 are not reachable, treat later PLC symbol read failures as runtime/router/permission failures rather than symbol-path failures.

Steps.Summary: Asserts that specific ADS ports are reachable and in the expected ADS state, turning activation false positives into hard failures.
Preconditions: TwinCAT runtime or router must be installed on the target machine.
Use after activation when a plan needs exact ports such as 10000, 200, and 300 to be Run.

Inputs.Name: NetId
Inputs.Description: AMS NetId, or local for the local router.

Inputs.Name: ExpectedPorts
Inputs.Description: Port/state assertions. Direct CLI accepts --expected=10000=Run;200=Run;300=Run, --ports=10000,200,300 --ads-state=Run, or --json-file.

Inputs.Name: DeviceState
Inputs.Description: Optional device state assertion per port.

Outputs.Name: succeededCount
Outputs.Description: Number of ports that matched the expected state.

Outputs.Name: failedCount
Outputs.Description: Number of ports that were unreachable or mismatched.

Outputs.Name: statesJson
Outputs.Description: Per-port expected and actual ADS/device state.

VerificationNotes: For OptCNC activation proof, require 10000=Run and system ports 200/300=Run instead of accepting a generic activation step success.

Steps.Summary: Asserts that no forbidden TcSysSrv Windows event-log entries appeared after a marker or within a recent time window.
Preconditions: Use after activation and ADS state checks.
By default it fails on Error/Critical TcSysSrv events and AdsState: >15< Config messages.

Inputs.Name: Marker
Inputs.Description: Inline marker returned by validation.mark-event-log-window.

Inputs.Name: MarkerFilePath
Inputs.Description: Marker JSON file written by validation.mark-event-log-window.

Inputs.Name: LogName
Inputs.Description: Windows event log name when no marker is supplied.

Inputs.Name: ProviderName
Inputs.Description: Event source/provider when no marker is supplied.

Inputs.Name: LookbackSeconds
Inputs.Description: Fallback lookback window when no marker is supplied.

Inputs.Name: FailOnErrorOrCritical
Inputs.Description: Whether Error/Critical entries fail the step.

Inputs.Name: FailOnConfigAdsState
Inputs.Description: Whether AdsState: >15< Config messages fail the step.

Inputs.Name: FailMessageContains
Inputs.Description: Additional message substrings that should fail the step.

Inputs.Name: MaxEvents
Inputs.Description: Maximum events to include in output; zero means no cap.

Outputs.Name: observedEventCount
Outputs.Description: Provider event count observed in the window.

Outputs.Name: errorOrCriticalCount
Outputs.Description: Error/Critical event count in the window.

Outputs.Name: configAdsStateCount
Outputs.Description: AdsState >15< Config message count in the window.

VerificationNotes: For OptCNC activation proof, this step closes the gap where engineering.activate-configuration succeeds but TcSysSrv immediately reports an error or returns to Config.

Steps.Summary: Asserts that no matching Windows Application crash events appeared after a marker or within a recent time window.
Preconditions: Use after Visual Studio/XAE launch, solution open, build, or activation when unattended runs must distinguish a COM/RPC disconnect from a crashed IDE host.
By default it checks Application Error, .NET Runtime, and Windows Error Reporting events for devenv.exe, TcXaeShell.exe, and TwinCAT System Manager modules.

Inputs.Name: Marker
Inputs.Description: Inline marker returned by validation.mark-event-log-window.

Inputs.Name: MarkerFilePath
Inputs.Description: Marker JSON file written by validation.mark-event-log-window.

Inputs.Name: LogName
Inputs.Description: Windows event log name.

Inputs.Name: LookbackSeconds
Inputs.Description: Fallback lookback window when no marker is supplied.

Inputs.Name: ProviderNames
Inputs.Description: Event sources to scan. Direct CLI accepts semicolon-separated provider names.

Inputs.Name: ProcessNames
Inputs.Description: Process names that should fail when found in an event message.

Inputs.Name: ModuleNames
Inputs.Description: Fault module names that should fail when found in an event message.

Inputs.Name: MessageContains
Inputs.Description: Additional message substrings that should fail the step.

Inputs.Name: MaxEvents
Inputs.Description: Maximum events to include in output; zero means no cap.

Outputs.Name: observedEventCount
Outputs.Description: Application event count observed in the window.

Outputs.Name: matchingEventCount
Outputs.Description: Crash event count matching the requested process/module/message filters.

VerificationNotes: For OptCNC, use the same marker file as the activation TcSysSrv event guard so a Visual Studio/XAE crash becomes an explicit validation failure instead of only an RPC error.

Steps.Summary: Marks the current Windows event-log position so a later step can assert only events from the same activation window.
Preconditions: Use immediately before engineering.activate-configuration.
This step is read-only and does not require Visual Studio or ADS.

Inputs.Name: LogName
Inputs.Description: Windows event log name.

Inputs.Name: ProviderName
Inputs.Description: Event source/provider to mark.

Inputs.Name: MarkerFilePath
Inputs.Description: Optional JSON file path where the marker should be written for a later plan step.

Outputs.Name: lastEntryIndex
Outputs.Description: Last observed provider event index at marker time.

VerificationNotes: For OptCNC, write the marker to evidenceDir before activation, then pass the marker file to validation.assert-event-log-window after ADS assertions.
